stories.headline: Trump Praises WHCA President Weijia Jiang After Shooting at Dinner
stories.summary: Weijia Jiang, a CBS correspondent, had been sitting next to the president at the White House Correspondents’ Association dinner when shots rang out. Soon afterward, she was reporting on the attack.

stories.headline: Security video appears to show suspect running towards White House correspondents' dinner
stories.summary: Security footage posted by President Donald Trump on social media appears to show the shooting suspect running toward White House Correspondents' dinner. Moments later, officials evacuated the area as gunshots were reported outside the ballroom.

stories.headline: Trump and officials 'likely' targets of press dinner shooting suspect, authorities believe
stories.summary: The suspect is expected to be charged with assault of a federal officer and using a firearm during a crime of violence, officials have said.

stories.headline: Abbas loyalists sweep Palestinian elections, including some seats in Gaza
stories.summary: Voter turnout in Gaza remained low due to the ongoing impact of the genocide.

stories.headline: What lies ahead for Gaza after ceasefires in Iran and Lebanon?
stories.summary: Regional de-escalation may shift focus to Gaza, where deadlock over Hamas weapons, future governance threatens 'truce'.

stories.headline: Trump says shooting incident shows why he needs new White House ballroom
stories.summary: President calls for legal action against $400mn project to be dropped after security breach at media dinner

stories.headline: Trump Evacuated After Shooting at Press Dinner, Gunman Detained
stories.summary: US authorities are charging a gunman in a shooting at the hotel that hosted the White House Correspondents’ Dinner in Washington, an incident that forced President Donald Trump to be rushed from the stage and the event to be postponed. Bloomberg News White House Correspondent Jeff Mason and State Department Reporter Eric Martin join David Gura and Christina Ruffini on Bloomberg This Weekend to discuss. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: After Shooting, Trump Demands Approval for His White House Ballroom
stories.summary: An incident involving an armed man at the White House correspondents’ dinner has the president to renewing his push for a project slowed by litigation.

stories.headline: What We Know About the Shooting at the White House Correspondents’ Dinner Trump Was Attending
stories.summary: A gunman carrying multiple weapons attempted to break into the dinner that President Trump was attending. Police have taken a suspect into custody.

stories.headline: Trump Describes His Mind-Set After Shooting at White House Correspondents’ Dinner
stories.summary: Very little was clear about what had happened at the Washington Hilton on Saturday night. But the president wanted to talk about it.

stories.headline: Live updates: White House correspondents’ dinner shooting suspect wrote statement denouncing Trump
stories.summary: The shooting prompted the evacuation of President Donald Trump and other high-profile guests from the White House Correspondents’ Association dinner Saturday. The suspect is in custody.

stories.headline: Gunman Was Tackled by Law Enforcement Near Correspondents’ Dinner Security Checkpoint
stories.summary: The gunman did not make it inside the hotel ballroom where President Trump, top officials and hundreds of journalists had gathered for dinner.

stories.headline: Who is Cole Allen? What we know about White House press dinner shooting suspect
stories.summary: The man arrested in the shooting at the White House correspondents' dinner, attended by US President Donald Trump, has been identified by US media as a mechanical engineer from California.

stories.headline: Moment Trump rushed to safety as gunshots heard at White House Correspondents' Dinner
stories.summary: US President Donald Trump was quickly escorted to safety from the White House Correspondents' Dinner after gunshots were heard at the venue.

stories.headline: Mali defence minister killed as country hit by wave of rebel attacks
stories.summary: A wave of coordinated attacks by jihadist militants and separatists has spread through the country.

stories.headline: Netanyahu orders army to 'vigorously attack' Hezbollah in Lebanon
stories.summary: The Israeli PM's directive comes as six people were killed by strikes in Lebanon despite a ceasefire being extended by three weeks.

stories.headline: Ukrainian action thriller billed as Saving Private Ryan for the drone age
stories.summary: Killhouse is based on real-life story of civilian couple saved from battlefield by Ukrainian drone operatorsIt is being billed as Ukraine’s answer to Saving Private Ryan, updated for an age of drones.The war movie Killhouse is an action thriller which shows off the latest in battlefield technology. Released this week, it features cameos by figures well known in Ukraine, including the nation’s former military intelligence chief Kyrylo Budanov. One

stories.headline: 40 years since the world’s worst nuclear disaster in Ukraine
stories.summary: 40 years since the world’s worst nuclear disaster in Ukraine

stories.headline: Mali defence minister killed after coordinated attacks
stories.summary: Mali’s Defence Minister Sadio Camara has been killed after coordinated attacks on military sites and his home.

stories.headline: Iran war tests role of Asian middle powers, says Thai ex-PM
Asia's great disconnect: Markets boom as Iran war fuels cost of living crisis
Europe fuel prices before the Iran war and after the ceasefire: Where did they rise most?
stories.summary: Fuel prices in Europe rose rapidly following the US–Israel strike on Iran and peaked shortly afterwards. While prices declined moderately after the ceasefire, both diesel and petrol remain notably higher than pre-strike levels.

stories.headline: Developer to fight £48mn government fire safety bill on human rights grounds
stories.summary: UK housing department is suing Urban Splash in test of powers to recoup public money spent on post-Grenfell building safety

stories.headline: Sen. Tillis Expected to Clear Way for Warsh as Fed Chair
stories.summary: Bloomberg's Michael McKee breaks down the big week ahead for the Fed, with Jerome Powell's final press conference on rate cuts this Wednesday as his term ends next month. Hours earlier, the Senate Banking Committee will move forward with confirming Kevin Warsh to be the next Fed Chair after the DOJ investigation was dropped. Sunday morning, Sen. Thom Tillis (R-North Carolina) said he would support Warsh's nomination. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Can the World’s Economic Firefighter Adapt to the 21st Century?
stories.summary: As wars, trade spats, and AI reshape the global economy, the IMF has held its role as the world’s lender-of-last-resort steady. Much of its governance, including countries’ shares of voting rights, have remained the same over decades, often requiring lending countries to return to the same austerity playbook. Gita Gopinath, former first managing director of the IMF says that the stability is by design in times of greater geopolitical uncertainty

stories.headline: Bomb attack on Colombia highway kills 19 ahead of election
stories.summary: Highway bombing in Colombia’s Cauca kills 19, injures dozens amid a surge in violence ahead of next month’s election.

stories.headline: Japan eyes expanded warship exports with Mogami-class frigate as template
New hotels in Japan: The standout openings to book in 2026
stories.summary: From Kyoto heritage stays to a converted prison in Nara, Japan’s newest hotels reflect a shift towards design, identity and experience-led travel.

stories.headline: Major City reforms to be set out in King’s Speech
stories.summary: Legislative package will include financial services bill designed to complete several planned changes to regulation

stories.headline: Benjamin Netanyahu’s biggest rivals merge Israeli political parties
stories.summary: Former prime ministers Naftali Bennett and Yair Lapid combine forces ahead of this year’s election

stories.headline: Goldman, JPMorgan Show Wall Street’s Split in Quantum Computing Race
stories.summary: As a breakthrough proves elusive in the quest to deploy the nascent technology and boost earnings, global finance is divided on how to proceed.

stories.headline: Russia’s Defense Minister Visits North Korea to Meet Top Leaders
stories.summary: Russia’s Defense Minister Andrey Belousov arrived in North Korea to meet political and military leaders as the two nations deepen strategic ties.

stories.headline: HK Finance Chief Says City’s 2026 IPOs Have Raised $17.9 Billion
stories.summary: Hong Kong initial public offerings have raised more than HK$140 billion ($17.9 billion) this year, with the city maintaining its position as the world’s top IPO venue, Financial Secretary Paul Chan wrote in his weekly blog.

The joy echoed across Hungary as people traded hugs and high-fives. stories.headline: Mali rattled by ongoing armed attacks: What to know
stories.summary: Mali's security situation has deteriorated in recent years, as armed groups have carried out coordinated attacks.

stories.summary: The elections, held in the Israeli-occupied West Bank and in Gaza's Deir al-Balah, are taking place against the backdrop of a highly volatile political and security environment.

stories.headline: Palestinians in West Bank and some in Gaza vote in local elections
stories.summary: Local elections have been held in the occupied West Bank and in one Gazan city, though Hamas and other groups are not taking part.

stories.headline: BBC visits Chernobyl ghost city 40 years after world's worst nuclear accident
stories.summary: The BBC's Jessica Parker visits Pripyat, which was abandoned in 1986 after an explosion at the nearby Chernobyl nuclear power plant.

stories.headline: Zelenskyy says he is ready to meet Putin in Azerbaijan
stories.summary: In a significant first visit to the South Caucasus since the start of Russia’s all-out war, Ukraine's President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said in a statement next to President Ilham Aliyev he is ready to meet Vladimir Putin in Azerbaijan after the US-led diplomatic talks have stalled over the past weeks.

stories.headline: Thirty-one sloths die in Florida before opening of attraction
stories.summary: Many died due to conditions at a warehouse they'd been shipped to, while others arrived dead, authorities say.

stories.headline: Two killed in strike on alleged drug boat in Pacific Ocean, US military says
stories.summary: The incident is the latest in a series of US strikes on alleged drug trafficking boats.

stories.headline: US military kills two more people in strike on alleged drug boat in Pacific

stories.headline: Russian strikes kill at least seven and injure dozens across Ukraine
stories.summary: Most of those killed and injured in Russian overnight attacks were in the eastern Ukrainian city of Dnipro, where an attack killed at least five and injured more than 30.

stories.headline: Trump cancels US envoys' trip to Pakistan for talks on Iran war
stories.summary: Iran had earlier said there were no plans for a direct meeting with a US delegation led by Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner.

stories.headline: Mexico says US agents killed in crash weren't permitted to operate there
stories.summary: The two Americans who reportedly worked for the CIA died in a car crash after a Mexican-led operation to destroy a drug lab.

stories.headline: Seven dead in major Russian attack on Ukraine
stories.summary: The city of Dnipro was hardest hit, with officials saying four died in a strike on a residential building.

stories.headline: Orbán steps down from Hungarian parliament after landslide defeat
stories.summary: The outgoing prime minister will not take up his seat after leading his party back into opposition.

stories.headline: The viral manifesto of 'anti-woke' tech boss with NHS and defence contracts
stories.summary: The boss of the controversial tech company with UK government contracts published a 22-point plan on the future of the West.

stories.headline: Katya Adler: Europe's Nato allies push back at reported US threat to Spain
stories.summary: Souring relations between Europe and the US have reared their Medusa-like head again, writes the BBC's Europe editor.

stories.headline: Malaysian Protesters Keep Up Pressure on Anti-Graft Chief
stories.summary: Malaysians gathered at a rally in Kuala Lumpur on Saturday, reiterating calls for the establishment of a Royal Commission of Inquiry into allegations involving outgoing anti-graft chief Azam Baki.

stories.headline: What counts as the woods? Judge axes Nova Scotia’s ban that defied ‘commonsense definitions’
stories.summary: The court sided with a Canadian hiker who deliberately challenged the order imposed to curb spread of wildfiresAs wildfires raged across Nova Scotia last summer, the Canadian province made a simple plea to residents: stay away from the woods.As the situation deteriorated, authorities turned the request into a prohibition: anyone caught hiking under the shade of the forest canopy faced a C$25,000 fine – a figure more than half the average worker’s

stories.headline: Key suspect in notorious Tadamon massacre during Syria civil war arrested
stories.summary: Amjad Youssef was wanted over the mass killing of blindfolded civilians in April 2013 in the district of Damascus.

stories.headline: Why is this game only legal across Australia one day a year?
stories.summary: Two-up is a coin toss betting game that was played by Australian soldiers during World War One - it’s now only legal across Australia on 25 April.

stories.headline: Woman trapped in poo for three hours after outback toilet collapses
stories.summary: The woman got stuck waist-deep in a pit latrine in the Australian outback after it gave way.

stories.headline: Philippines' Duterte to stand trial as ICC confirms crimes against humanity charges

stories.summary: In a memo, the Department of Justice says the move will "strengthen" the death penalty and deter crime.

stories.summary: Piracy, once notorious in this stretch of the Indian Ocean, has made a comeback in recent years.

stories.headline: Headscarf with a beret: Muslim designers showcase floral dresses and boxy streetwear in Paris
stories.summary: Some young attendees say the event demonstrated a more inclusive French culture taking shape.

stories.headline: Israel's Benjamin Netanyahu treated for early-stage prostate cancer
stories.summary: The Israeli prime minister says he is "in excellent physical condition" after having a malignant tumour removed.

stories.headline: The Kashmir town trying to win back tourists after a deadly attack
stories.summary: A year after the Pahalgam attack, tourism has slowed in Indian-administered Kashmir, hitting lives and livelihoods.

stories.headline: Why police are seeking to arrest billionaire K-pop mogul behind BTS
stories.summary: Bang Si-hyuk, who created the supergroup, denies defrauding investors before his $7.3bn company went public.

stories.headline: Israeli strikes kill eight Palestinians in Gaza, first responders say
stories.summary: One strike killed three children and two adults near a mosque in the northern town of Beit Lahia on Wednesday evening.

stories.summary: The polls are taking place against a controversy over a sweeping revision of electoral rolls.

stories.summary: Barry speaks with David Gardner, Author and Co-Founder, at The Motley Fool. They discuss David's new book "Rule Breaker Investing: How to Pick the Best Stocks of the Future and Build Lasting Wealth." They also discuss the pros and cons of different investing strategies and trends. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Heatwaves, floods and wildfires pose rising threat to democracy, report finds
stories.summary: Research shows natural hazards linked to climate crisis disrupted 23 elections in 18 countries in 2024Democracy is under mounting threat from the climate crisis, with new analysis documenting how elections are increasingly shaped not only by political forces but also by floods, wildfires and extreme weather.At least 94 elections and referendums across 52 countries have been disrupted by climate-related impacts over the last two decades, researche

stories.headline: Moldovan oligarch jailed in $1bn 'theft of the century' case
stories.summary: Vlad Plahotniuc, once Moldova's richest man, was found guilty of partaking in a fraud which involved around 12% of the country's GDP.

stories.headline: Hotpot, bubble tea and sportswear: China's new exports take on the world
stories.summary: Made in China used to mean cheap, mass-produced goods but a new wave of Chinese brands is emerging.

stories.headline: India has splurged billions on metro trains. But where are the commuters?
stories.summary: Without better last-mile connectivity and affordable fares, metro use is unlikely to improve quickly, say experts.
